source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
An object with mass $m=2\mathrm{kg}$ is subjected to a horizontal pulling force $F$ and starts from rest to undergo uniformly accelerated linear motion on a smooth horizontal surface. After a time $t=6\mathrm{s}$, the displacement is $x=54\mathrm{m}$.

1. The magnitude of the average velocity $v$ of the object during this time period
2. The magnitude of the acceleration of the object $a$
3. The magnitude of the horizontal pulling force $F$.

image_dependency: question is fully specified by textual physics quantities and formulas
multi_step: The problem asks for three outputs: average velocity, acceleration, and force
Solving later parts depends on earlier or intermediate kinematics results from displacement, time, and start-from-rest conditions
Requires combining uniformly accelerated motion with Newton's second law
No image or external context is needed, but the solution is not single-step retrieval
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with explicit numeric answers and units
Given values (m=2 kg, t=6 s, x=54 m, starts from rest, smooth horizontal surface) are sufficient to recompute the results
Each requested quantity can be checked by standard equations: average velocity=x/t, x=(1/2)at^2, F=ma
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present

decision_reasons: 题干和答案资产完整，question_present=true 且 answer_present=true，没有登记问题
